spec_match.rationale: The page explains and models CSS precedence over the pathLength presentation attribute, but its animation moves stroke-dashoffset rather than demonstrating interpolation or transition of path-length itself.

interactive.rationale: The attribute range, CSS on/off controls, and play/pause control all update their rendered comparison and textual readouts, with a clear pre-150 fallback.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The cascade promise is delivered, but the feature card explicitly says path-length animates with keyframes and transition while the concept only animates stroke-dashoffset.

edge_cases.rationale: Unsupported-browser fallback is handled cleanly, but specificity, inheritance, invalid values, and path-length interpolation endpoints are not exercised.

references.rationale: The page links directly to ChromeStatus feature 4861677550043136 and the SVG specification pull request.

conformance.rationale: A feature-level conformance suite now exists (v150/support-path-length-as-a-css-property/conformance.json), verified to execute in headless Chromium.

runtime_evidence.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP in Headless Chrome 149 exercised the attribute slider, both cascade buttons, and play/pause; DOM readouts changed, animation-play-state became running, resources and telemetry POST succeeded, and no console errors appeared.

telemetry_health.rationale: The shared telemetry client loaded and POSTed successfully, but control-specific cascade and animation interactions were not exposed as inspectable events during review.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared color variables, pairs button background and foreground overrides, and provides multiple genuine interactive comparisons.

accessibility.rationale: Native controls are keyboard-focusable and named, but mode and play buttons do not expose aria-pressed state, the visible slider label is not associated, and dynamic readouts are not live regions.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate path-length interpolation itself
openQuestions.detail: Add a concept that animates or transitions path-length between numeric values and shows its computed value and visible dash/textPath consequence. The current animation changes stroke-dashoffset, so it does not substantiate the page and feature-card claim that path-length itself is animatable.

openQuestions.title: Expose toggle state and live results to assistive technology
openQuestions.detail: Add aria-pressed to cascade and animation controls, associate the slider's visible label with its input, and announce effective-total and playback changes with restrained status semantics.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP tested http://localhost:3001/v150/support-path-length-as-a-css-property/css-overrides-attribute/ in Headless Chrome 149. The attribute slider changed 500 to 1200 and updated the comparison; CSS off/on changed the effective total between 2000 and 1000; play changed animation-play-state to running. The page honestly reported the SVG attribute fallback because path-length is not supported in Chrome 149. Console was clean and all resources plus the telemetry POST succeeded. The accessibility tree exposed named native controls but no pressed state on the stateful buttons. The feature conformance route has no suite. Screenshot: /tmp/showcase-v150-css-overrides.png.
